title: MCA Annual Industry Report 2026
gist: Oxford Economics' 2026 MCA Annual Industry Report estimates the UK consulting market at roughly £21.8bn, with MCA member fee income growing an average of 3% in 2025 amid a sharply bifurcated sector (52% growing, 46% declining), while export earnings grew 9% and outpaced both domestic growth and the wider UK business services sector's 6% export growth.

insights.claim: MCA member firms generated £10.9bn in fee income in 2025, per the joint Oxford Economics/MCA Annual Industry Report 2026.
insights.speaker: Suki Masana
insights.so_what: Provides a hard revenue benchmark for the UK's largest consulting firms, useful for sizing addressable market and comparing firm-level performance against the industry aggregate.
insights.quote: MCA member firms generated £10.9bn in fee income in 2025.
insights.locator: Some of the standout results are that MCA member firms

insights.claim: Because MCA membership represents around half of the UK consulting industry, the total UK consulting market is estimated at approximately £21.8bn in 2025.
insights.speaker: Suki Masana
insights.so_what: Gives investors and allocators a top-down estimate of total UK consulting industry revenue, derived by doubling the MCA member figure — useful as a market-sizing anchor but sensitive to the accuracy of the 'half of industry' assumption.
insights.quote: As MCA membership represents around half of the UK consulting industry, this suggests that the total size of the market is approximately £21.8bn.
insights.locator: As MCA membership represents around half

insights.claim: Average fee income across MCA member firms that reported comparable data in both years increased by 3% in 2025 versus 2024, but this headline figure masks a highly split performance: 52% of members grew, 1% were unchanged, and 46% saw fee income decline.
insights.speaker: Suki Masana
insights.so_what: Nearly half of consulting firms are shrinking even as the sector average shows modest growth, signalling a bifurcated market where firm selection matters more than broad sector exposure.
insights.quote: 52% of members reported growth; 1% remained unchanged; and 46% recorded a decline.
insights.locator: This overall figure masks a mixed performance

insights.claim: Export earnings among MCA member firms with comparable 2024-2025 data grew 9%, exceeding domestic fee income growth and outpacing the wider UK business services sector, which recorded export growth of 6% over the same period.
insights.speaker: Suki Masana
insights.so_what: UK consulting firms are winning international demand faster than the broader UK business services sector, suggesting relative competitiveness of UK consulting expertise abroad and a potential outperformance signal versus peer service exporters.
insights.quote: export earnings increased by 9%, exceeding the growth in domestic fee income and outpacing the wider UK business services sector, which recorded growth of 6% over the same period
insights.locator: Among MCA member firms providing comparable data

insights.claim: Exports accounted for 31% of total MCA member fee income in 2025, a modest increase on the prior year, indicating UK consulting revenue is becoming increasingly internationally oriented.
insights.speaker: Suki Masana
insights.so_what: Rising export share signals growing dependence on overseas demand for UK consulting revenue, a factor to monitor against currency moves and global client budgets.
insights.quote: Exports accounted for 31% of total fee income in 2025, a modest increase on the previous year.
insights.locator: Exports accounted for 31% of total fee income

insights.claim: Export exposure is concentrated rather than broad-based: only 61% of MCA member firms reported any overseas income in 2025, and among those firms overseas income averaged just 28% of total revenue.
insights.speaker: Suki Masana
insights.so_what: The strong 9% export growth headline masks the fact that nearly 4 in 10 member firms have no overseas revenue at all, meaning export strength is driven by a subset of internationally exposed firms rather than the whole membership.
insights.quote: In 2025, 61% of firms reported generating income from overseas markets with an average total share income of 28%.
